No. Notified Amount (₹ crore) Tenor (day) Window Timing Date of Reversal 1 50,000 1 09:30 AM to 10:00 AM July 08, 2026 (Wednesday) 2. items.title: SBI MF set to enter private credit, unlisted equity via AIF
items.summary: RBI greenlight paves the way for the fund house to tap India’s $25-billion private credit market

items.title: Wall Street edges higher as chip stocks rebound
items.summary: As of 9:35 a.m. Eastern Time, the S&P 500 rose 0.4%, the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.8%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up or 0.2%

items.title: AI investors may pivot to hyperscalers from chipmakers, Morgan Stanley says
items.summary: Morgan Stanley has recommended a strategic pivot in market investments, directing attention from semiconductor stocks to AI-based giants such as Alphabet and Amazon. This shift is fueled by expectations of reduced rate hikes and a decline in oil prices, which favor sectors like consumer discretionary, transport, and biotech. As major players invest significantly in AI infrastructure, investors are seeking tangible outcomes, potentially signaling a downturn for chip stocks.

items.title: Microsoft Layoffs: Xbox cuts 4,800 jobs and plans to spin off four gaming studios
items.summary: The ongoing restructuring at Microsoft is set to impact hundreds of developers across some of the gaming industry's most respected mid-tier studios.

items.title: Trump rings Wall Street's opening bells as he ties his presidency to stock market gains
items.summary: President Trump symbolically opened the stock market from the Oval Office, linking his presidency to market gains amid rising inflation. Despite claims of record-setting markets, only 33% approve of his economic leadership. The initiative promotes "Trump Accounts" for children's investments, highlighting that many families lack direct stock exposure, a benefit often seen in wealthier households. Inflation, however, continues to impact public approval.

items.title: Saudi Arabia cuts oil prices for Asia customers by $11 a barrel in biggest reduction in 26 years
items.summary: Saudi Arabia cut the price of its main crude grade for customers in Asia in August by the most in at least 26 years, as a surge of global supply heightens competition for buyers.

items.title: The mental health crisis tea garden executives have long faced, now a study explores it
items.summary: A groundbreaking doctoral study by Dr. Pransu Raj Kaushik, awarded by Dibrugarh University, has illuminated the critical yet overlooked issue of workplace stress and mental health among tea garden executives. The research, a comparative analysis involving NETA member companies and BCPL, offers vital insights for improving employee well-being and productivity within the tea sector.

items.title: Belgium set to appeal as FIFA reverses Balogun's World Cup suspension after Trump reportedly intervenes
items.summary: Folarin Balogun was surprisingly cleared by FIFA to play for the United States Men's National Team in the World Cup against Belgium on Monday.

items.title: India's office leasing hits record as multinationals expand despite global uncertainty
items.summary: In the first half of 2026, India's office market saw unprecedented growth, with a staggering 45.5 million square feet leased. This remarkable rise was propelled by multinational firms, especially those enhancing their Global Capability Centres, contributing to 43% of total leasing. Despite global challenges, India stands out as an attractive locale owing to its talent pool, cost benefits, and property availability.

items.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs, as Xbox unit downsizes and plans to spin off four gaming studios
items.summary: Microsoft is cutting jobs in its commercial business and its Xbox gaming group, where revenue has been shrinking.

items.title: Indian Railways revises penalties! Avoid these costly mistakes
Prayagraj records over 20% unemployment rate among 46 surveyed cities with 10 lakh+ population: Report
items.summary: Prayagraj and Patna recorded over 20% unemployment rates among 46 major cities, while Ahmedabad and Kolkata had the lowest at 2.5%. The average unemployment rate stands at 6.8%.

items.title: NATO tensions are ‘growing pains,’ U.S. ambassador says as Trump presses allies
items.summary: U.S. Ambassador to NATO Matthew Whitaker said NATO's allies boosting defense spending under pressure from Trump reflects “growing pains,” not a crisis.

items.title: 'Electronic warfare is a tech phenomenon': Why the market is rethinking defense valuations
items.summary: There's a growing investment in newer areas of defense such as deep strike capabilities, anti-drone and unmanned systems, with different priorities for different countries.

items.title: US stocks today: US stocks open higher as chip shares recover
items.summary: Wall Street opened mixed on Monday as chip stocks rebounded, extending last week’s rally. While the Nasdaq and S&P 500 gained at the open, the Dow slipped slightly. Investors are now focused on upcoming Federal Reserve minutes and the start of the second-quarter earnings season for further market direction.

items.title: Policy, energy, climate, geopolitics to influence global veg oil sector: IVPA
items.summary: They are increasingly influencing production, trade flows and price formation, says Sudhakar Desai, the association president

items.title: India office market defies global turmoil with GCC-led leasing
items.summary: The office market logged record H1 2026 leasing of 45.5 million sq ft, led by global capability centres and flex space operators even as geopolitical tensions weighed on sentiment. GCCs alone accounted for 43% of demand, with CBRE expecting them to drive over 40% of full-year absorption.

items.title: PE, M&A deal values surge despite global conflicts on big-ticket cross-border transactions
items.summary: Overall deal value jumps 127 per cent in Q2 to $36.3 billion even as volumes decline

items.title: Future skills for kids: Why AI, financial literacy and communication are becoming essential for students
items.summary: The world children are growing up in is changing at an unprecedented pace. New technologies are transforming the way people learn, work, and solve problems. Careers are evolving faster than ever, and the skills needed to succeed are expanding well beyond traditional academics.

items.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore in June
items.summary: Indian companies significantly boosted their fundraising via commercial papers in June, reaching a near five-year high. This surge, driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing, saw Rs 2.53 lakh crore raised. Experts note a shift from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding, supported by ample investor demand and a stable liquidity environment. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

items.title: Air India lists six A319 aircraft for sale through Skytech-AIC
items.summary: Air India is offloading six Airbus A319 aircraft, built between 2003 and 2006, as part of its fleet rationalisation. Skytech-AIC, a UK-based firm, has been appointed to manage the sale of these narrowbody jets, which will be offered without their engines. This move aligns with Air India's ongoing fleet transformation and modernisation strategy under the Tata Group, as the airline phases out older planes to make way for new-generation aircraft.

items.title: Rentomojo gets Sebi's approval to float IPO
items.summary: Rentomojo, an innovative online rental platform specializing in furniture and appliances, has received approval from Sebi for its Initial Public Offering (IPO). Aiming to raise up to Rs 150 crore through new equity and an Offer for Sale by current shareholders, the proceeds will support loan repayments, warehouse rentals, and overall business operations. With a strong subscriber base and diverse product offerings in various cities, Rentomojo is poised for growth.

items.title: Why AI fluency will define the next generation of business leaders
items.summary: AI is reshaping every business function, but most organisations lack the practical knowledge to use it effectively. ET Masterclasses help leaders, founders, marketers and investors build real-world AI fluency for confident, strategic decision-making.

items.title: Lockheed Martin to buy naval defense firm Ultra Maritime for $3.45 billion
items.summary: Ultra is owned by private equity firm Advent International, and specialises in anti-submarine technology.

items.title: Versant agrees to buy golf simulator company Full Swing for $530 million
items.summary: The deal for Full Swing will expand Versant's nontraditional media assets in an effort to diversify revenue from cable television.

items.title: Can Zerodha's 'nice place to work' culture become its real moat? Here's what Nithin Kamath says
items.summary: Nithin Kamath said Zerodha’s culture stems from deliberate choices, including staying small, avoiding revenue targets and prioritising employee well-being over growth. He stressed patient hiring, learning from mistakes and resisting incentives that encourage excessive customer trading or employee burnout.

items.title: Data center provider Csquare targets up to $4.18 billion valuation in US IPO
items.summary: The company said on Monday it aims ​to raise ‌up to $1.35 billion in the IPO by offering 50 million ‌shares at $23 to $27 apiece

items.title: India's wellness industry poised for growth amid focus on preventive healthcare, rising income: EBG Group chief
items.summary: India's wellness market, valued at over $150 billion, is set for growth fueled by rising health consciousness and disposable incomes. EBG Group is launching Carlton Wellness India, an integrated ecosystem blending traditional Indian healing with modern hospitality. This platform will offer diverse wellness experiences, from city centers to retreats, emphasizing preventive care and holistic wellbeing for everyday life.

items.title: IndianOil LNG gets green nod for ₹3,400-crore Ennore LNG terminal expansion
items.summary: The project will double the terminal’s regasification capacity from 5 MTPA to 10 MTPA, increasing gas supply to industries, power plants, fertiliser units and city gas networks across Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka

items.title: Valuation Watch: 12 mid-cap stocks with sky-high PE ratios
items.summary: These 12 NSE mid-cap stocks are trading at TTM P/E ratios above 100x, making them among the market's most richly valued names.

items.title: Quote of the day by Fred Schwed Jr: "Most financial principles and theories have a degree of good sense to them. It may be a large degree, but it never comes close to being absolute."
items.summary: Financial theories offer valuable guidance but are never absolute, as Fred Schwed Jr. wisely noted. From diversification to valuation, every principle has its limits, often challenged by market psychology and unforeseen events. Successful investors embrace flexibility, adapting to changing conditions rather than rigidly adhering to fixed assumptions. This open-minded approach, balancing proven frameworks with a willingness to learn, is crucial for navigating today's dynamic financial landscape.

items.title: Govt relaxes verification norms for high capacity weighing instruments
items.summary: The government has significantly eased rules for verifying large weighing instruments, those with a capacity of one tonne or more. This move aims to boost business ease by reducing the need for heavy standard weights during checks. The revised regulations now require only 20% of the instrument's maximum capacity in standard weights, down from 50%, streamlining operations and cutting costs for industries and weighbridge owners.

items.title: Taking on HUL, P&G and Marico: Why Honasa wants to be more than just Mamaearth
items.summary: A few years ago, Mamaearth rewrote the beauty playbook. Now, with shifting consumer tastes and intense competition, the brand’s parent, Honasa Consumer, is looking to reinvent itself with a broader portfolio. How that plays out will determine if it's a one-brand wonder or grows as something bigger.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P, Nasdaq futures jump up to 1% as tech stocks recover; crude oil extends decline
items.summary: US stock futures rose, led by gains in tech shares and falling crude prices. Nasdaq 100 futures rose 1%, while the Dow remained stable. Investors await critical Federal Reserve meeting minutes and earnings reports from major companies, including Delta Air Lines and PepsiCo.

items.title: Prestige Estates buys 50% stake in Advent Convention and Hotels International for Rs 504 crore
items.summary: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd is set to enhance its market presence by securing a 50% stake in Advent Convention and Hotels International Ltd for Rs 504 crore. This deliberate acquisition will pave the way for a major commercial development in Mumbai's Andheri East, featuring 1.50 million sq ft of leasable area and a total development worth Rs 4,500 crore. This investment reflects Prestige's ambitious growth plans.

items.title: Reliance Industries gets Sebi warning over employee trades during UPSI period
items.summary: Sebi issued an administrative warning to Reliance Industries after finding two employees and a relative traded while possessing unpublished price-sensitive information. The regulator flagged compliance lapses, but imposed no financial or operational restrictions, with Reliance promising corrective measures to strengthen monitoring.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | US Stock Market Today | Live: Trump rings Wall Street opening bell, links presidency to stock market performance
Ather Energy Is said to plan $200 million India share sale
items.summary: The electric two-wheeler maker has appointed HSBC Holdings Plc, Axis Capital Ltd. and Nomura Holdings Inc. to manage the qualified institutional placement

items.title: Indian bond yields slide on rain relief, continued foreign demand
items.summary: Indian government bond yields continued their downward trend on Monday, boosted by improving monsoon rains and consistent foreign investment. The benchmark 10-year yield closed below a significant technical level, signaling potential for further gains. Overseas investors have been actively buying Indian bonds, attracted by measures to support currency inflows. Meanwhile, domestic swap rates also eased as monsoon concerns lessened and the likelihood of a domestic rate hike diminished.

items.title: ITV hits such as I'm a Celebrity to stay free to watch after Sky takeover
items.summary: Sky boss Dana Strong's comments came as the channel announces it is buying ITV's media and entertainment divisions in a £1.6bn deal.

items.title: The supertanker tycoon making millions on Hormuz shuttle runs
items.summary: Sinokor Group’s ships traveled ‘dark’ without their transponders (and often under the cover of literal darkness) before offloading their cargo into other tankers waiting outside the waterway, and then returning back to collect more

items.title: Sensex jumps 521 points, Nifty closes above 24,400 on banking, realty gains
items.summary: Sensex and Nifty have gained over 2% in the last four trading sessions

items.title: 'Treat EVs like gadgets': Tata Electric Mobility CCO Vivek Srivatsa on resale fears, Sierra EV, price parity with ICE and why Gen Z is the next big bet
items.summary: In an exclusive interview with ET, Vivek Srivatsa, Chief Commercial Officer of Tata Passenger Electric Mobility, speaks about the Sierra EV's target audience, the resale value challenge, Battery-as-a-Service, Gen Zs as the next wave of buyers, and Tata's road to closing the price gap between EVs and their petrol equivalents.

items.title: OPEC+ to prioritise oil supply over price floor until Hormuz normalises; Brent likely to stay below $85: Analyst
items.summary: Kotak Securities expects the rupee to appreciate to 92-93 against the U.S. dollar by September, with RBI intervention likely to curb further gains

items.title: Britain's FTSE indexes slip as miners offset financial gains
items.summary: London's FTSE 100 saw a slight dip as precious metal miners declined, countered by gains in financial stocks. EasyJet surged on a sweetened takeover bid, while ITV advanced after agreeing to sell its media division. Investors are also watching for potential boosts to the UK government bond market from the Bank of England, amidst ongoing geopolitical considerations.

items.title: EL Nino to hit India's energy system harder than anywhere else in world: CREA report
items.summary: India's energy system faces a significant El Nino impact, with reduced renewable power generation and increased demand for air conditioning. A CREA analysis predicts a potential 18 TWh generation gap, likely leading to a surge in coal-fired power and substantial CO2 emissions. The report stresses the urgency of accelerating solar and battery deployment to bolster grid resilience against extreme weather.

items.title: Sterlite Technologies raises ₹1,500 cr via QIP to fund next phase of growth
items.summary: Following the allotment, STL's paid-up equity share capital increased to ₹102.78 crore, comprising 51.39 crore equity shares

items.title: Not just Elon Musk; retail investors are losing money in Tesla and SpaceX crash. What should you do?
items.summary: The sharp correction in Tesla and SpaceX has dented retail investors' portfolios, including those of Indians investing in US stocks. Experts say the selloff reflects a broader repricing of AI-linked stocks and advise investors to review portfolio allocation, risk appetite and investment horizon instead of reacting to short-term volatility.

items.title: IndusInd Bank among 6 stocks to hit 52-week highs & rally up to 25% in a month
items.summary: Six BSE 100 stocks, including Cholamandalam Investment, IndusInd Bank and Lupin, touched fresh 52-week highs as the Sensex climbed 521 points. The milestone reflects strong buying interest, positive momentum and growing investor confidence amid the broader market rally.

items.title: Sentosa eyes higher tourist inflows from India as arrivals to Singapore island rise 8% in FY26
items.summary: Sentosa Development Corporation is ramping up initiatives to woo Indian tourists, a vital demographic fueling the island's tourism growth. Following an impressive 8% annual increase in visitors from India, the island seeks to attract even more wanderers. Additionally, the forthcoming integration of Sentosa and Brani islands provides a prime opportunity for Indian businesses eager to explore international markets.

items.title: How SpaceX investors minted money before and after historic $75 billion IPO on Binance
items.summary: Binance highlighted how eligible users gained exposure to SpaceX before, during and after its record IPO through pre-IPO perpetuals, direct stock trading and tokenised bStocks. The exchange said the multi-stage approach drove strong trading volumes, liquidity and investor participation across different asset classes.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Vinati Organics (Accumulate)
items.summary: CD Research

items.title: Wegovy weight loss pill now available in UK - here's what you need to know
items.summary: The once-a-day pill, from the makers of the Wegovy weight-loss jab, can now be bought privately in UK pharmacies.

items.title: Karnataka: Govt blocking Centre's HMT revival plans as package gets ready, says HD Kumaraswamy
items.summary: Union Minister HD Kumaraswamy has accused the Karnataka government of obstructing the revival of HMT by using the forest department to claim 430 acres of its land. He alleges this move, coinciding with a special revival package nearing finalization, is an attempt to interfere with judicial proceedings and benefit vested interests. Kumaraswamy vowed to challenge the order and assured HMT employees of the revival under Prime Minister Modi's leadership.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Sobha (Buy)
items.summary: JM Financial

items.title: Delhi High Court orders Chinese smartphone maker Transsion to furnish security in InterDigital patent case
items.summary: Shenzhen opposed the request for a security deposit, arguing that InterDigital had failed to establish, even on a prima facie basis, the validity and essentiality of the patents in question

items.title: HDFC Bank shares jump 3.6% on strong Q1FY27 business update
items.summary: Period-end deposits stood at about ₹31,70,500 crore as of June 30, 2026, compared with ₹27,64,100 crore a year earlier, reflecting a growth of around 14.7 per cent y-o-y

items.title: Aastha Spintex shares end flat after 4.4% discount debut
items.summary: Aastha Spintex’s muted listing does not overshadow the company’s strong financial turnaround, an analyst said

items.title: Indian IT earnings preview: AI, weak demand and geopolitics cloud outlook
items.summary: Currency gains are expected to mask weak operational performance, as India’s leading IT companies are likely to post constant-currency revenue growth of under 3% in the June quarter

items.title: EasyJet shares rally 10% on potential $7.3 billion acquisition by Castlelake
items.summary: EasyJet shares jumped nearly 10% after the airline reached an agreement in principle with US investment firm Castlelake over a potential $7.3 billion acquisition. Castlelake's revised offer of £6.90 per share represents a nearly 24% premium to easyJet's previous closing price.

items.title: Trump's calls, Ukraine's strikes and Russia's barrage on Kyiv put markets on alert
items.summary: Trump reportedly spoke with Putin and Zelenskyy as Ukraine struck Russian targets and Moscow launched another deadly attack on Kyiv.

items.title: Jindal Stainless appoints former Adani Energy executive Kunjal Mehta as CFO
items.summary: Jindal Stainless, India's leading stainless steel maker, has appointed Kunjal Mehta, formerly of Adani Energy Solutions, as its new Chief Financial Officer, effective June 25. Mehta, a seasoned finance professional with extensive experience in corporate finance and value creation, is expected to bolster the company's financial strength and growth. Jindal Stainless is currently expanding its production capacity and global reach.

items.title: Rupee slips 20 paise as stronger dollar offsets gains from lower crude prices
items.summary: While positive domestic equities, lower crude oil prices and renewed foreign investor inflows offered support, expectations of another US rate hike kept the dollar firm

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex jumps 521 points, Nifty closes above 24,430
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: The Sensex rose 521.16 points (0.67%) to close at 78,285.07, while the Nifty gained 159.50 points (0.66%) to end the day at 24,430.35.

items.title: India at cusp of 'snacking renaissance'; L Catterton bets on healthy snacking, branded foods
items.summary: Expressing enthusiasm for India's rising consumption trends, L Catterton zeroes in on the healthy snacking and branded foods market. Having launched its first fund dedicated to India, the firm has made three key investments lately. Executive Chairman Sanjiv Mehta emphasizes the importance of transitioning from unbranded to branded products, predicting a resurgence in Indian snacking habits while calling for heightened investments in food innovation to cultivate globally recognized brands.

items.title: How brands reach business leaders: The power of credibility, context, and visibility
items.summary: Business leaders discover brands through trusted environments, meaningful industry conversations, and credible platforms, not just advertisements. Explore why context has become a competitive advantage for organisations looking to build influence among India's key decision-makers.

items.title: EasyJet shares soar 10% as budget airline agrees to $7.3 billion Castlelake takeover
items.summary: EasyJet shares surged in early dealmaking Monday after it agreed in principle to takeover bid from Castlelake.

items.title: Blinkit takes the premium groceries route with ‘Gourmet’ to lift order values
items.summary: Blinkit’s premium pilot operates through dedicated dark stores featuring niche brands, signalling the quick-commerce leader’s latest push to capture affluent shoppers and strengthen its competitive edge.

items.title: Rupee hits three-week low at 95.39 vs USD as firmer dollar, NDF maturities pinch
items.summary: The Indian rupee touched a three-week low against the dollar on Monday, influenced by a stronger greenback and demand for foreign currency. While falling oil prices and measures to boost dollar inflows offered some support, corporate arbitrage trades added to the pressure. Analysts at Goldman Sachs have revised their rupee forecasts, anticipating a stronger performance. Global markets saw stocks rise as oil prices eased, potentially curbing inflation.

items.title: TPG acquires Aseem Infrastructure from NIIF with GIC, ICICI backing
items.summary: Global private equity firm TPG is acquiring a full stake in Aseem Infrastructure Finance, a leading sustainable infrastructure debt financier. Partnering with Singapore's GIC and ICICI Bank, TPG's climate investing platform will take over the company, which has funded significant renewable energy and power transmission projects. Aseem Infrastructure, founded by NIIF, has disbursed over Rs 40,000 crore in loans, contributing to substantial greenhouse gas emission reductions.

items.title: Global Market: Citi joins London's bullion clearing network in major precious metals expansion
items.summary: Citi has joined London's over-the-counter gold market clearing network, becoming the fifth bank to do so. This significant move expands the bullion trade's settlement infrastructure, which handles approximately $160 billion in daily transactions. Citi's entry, the first in a decade, signals progress in making the market more accessible. The bank will now offer Loco London settlement for gold, silver, platinum, and palladium.

items.title: TPG, GIC and ICICI Bank acquire Aseem Infrastructure Finance for ₹5,000 crore
items.summary: Aseem is a leading sustainable infrastructure debt financier with a core focus on renewable energy, power transmission and other infrastructure assets in India. It was established in 2020 by the National Investment and Infrastructure Fund, India’s sovereign-anchored alternative asset manager.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 6 July: Swan Corp, Zen Tech, HFCL, HDFC Bank, Thermax, Hitachi Energy among top gainers
items.summary: Indian stock markets rose on July 6, with benchmark indices gaining over 0.60%. The Nifty 50 closed 0.66% higher at 24,420, while Sensex advanced 0.67% to 78,236. HDFC Bank and Axis Bank drove gains, while Nifty Realty led sectoral increases with a 2% rise.

items.title: Indian Oil, HPCL buy 7 million barrels of crude via tenders, trade sources say
items.summary: Indian refiners Indian Oil Corp and Hindustan Petroleum Corp have collectively secured approximately 7 million barrels of crude oil through recent tenders. Indian Oil acquired various grades from Angola and Nigeria, while HPCL purchased Brazilian Tupi crude. These significant purchases are slated for delivery between August and September, bolstering India's energy reserves.

items.title: Stock market today: Sensex jumps 500 points, Nifty 50 closes above 24,400; investors earn ₹2 lakh crore in a day
items.summary: The Sensex ended 521 points, or 0.67%, higher at 78,285, while the Nifty 50 closed at 24,430, up 160 points, or 0.66%. The mid and small-cap segments also rose. The Nifty Midcap 100 and Smallcap 100 indices gained 0.45% and 0.75%, respectively.

items.title: Apple veteran's Chinese smart-glasses firm becomes unicorn as Tencent, Meituan fund rival to Meta
items.summary: The Shenzhen-based startup joined a slew of global peers to build advanced gadgets and electronics that bring the benefits of AI to individual users.

items.title: Sebi aims to ease shorting by nearly doubling stocks eligible for borrowing: Report
items.summary: India's market regulator is set to nearly double the number of stocks available for short selling, aiming to invigorate the cash market and steer investors from riskier derivatives. Collateral requirements are also slated for reduction. These moves follow past tightening of rules and seek to include more liquid shares, with final details expected by year-end.

items.title: Gold slips ₹150, silver drops ₹5,000 as strong US dollar dents bullion demand
items.summary: Traders said the market remained range-bound as investors balanced lingering geopolitical risks in West Asia against a stronger greenback and expectations surrounding the US Federal Reserve's monetary policy.

items.title: Ashish Kacholia, Madhusudan Kela lead ace investors’ comeback after March rout
items.summary: The median portfolio of ace investors, those with holdings of more than ₹1,000 crore each, likely stood at 18.7% in the June quarter, compared with a median fall of 15.6% in the March quarter.

items.title: Who is Maya Tata? Noel Tata's daughter to drive Westside’s e-commerce push
items.summary: Maya Tata has actively transitioned across different businesses within the Tata conglomerate. She began her career in finance at Tata Capital before moving to Tata Digital

items.title: EasyJet agrees 'in principle' to £5.2bn takeover deal
items.summary: The low-cost airline had previously rejected four takeover offers from US investment firm Castlelake.

items.title: Raymond shares rise 4% after former BEL chief joins defence business
items.summary: Raymond shares rose nearly 4% after the company appointed former Bharat Electronics Chairman and Managing Director Bhanu Prakash Srivastava as CEO of its defence business. The company expects his nearly four decades of experience in defence technologies and programme execution to strengthen its expansion into defence electronics, systems integration, aerospace and related engineering businesses.

items.title: Sunsure Energy secures Rs 262 cr project financing from FMO, Axis Bank
items.summary: Sunsure Energy has secured a significant Rs 262 crore financing for its 75 MWp open-access solar project in Tamil Nadu. Dutch development bank FMO is leading the funding, with Axis Bank contributing approximately Rs 71 crore. This marks FMO's debut in India's Group Captive C&I renewable energy sector, aiming to power commercial and industrial clients with clean energy.

items.title: Asian Paints declares board meeting date to announce Q1 results 2026
items.summary: Asian Paints Ltd. 's board of directors will meet on July 29, 2026, to review and approve the audited financial results for the quarter ended 30 June 2026.

items.title: Gene therapy firm Immuneel ties up with Arogya Finance to offer Rs 25 lakh zero-cost EMI for cancer treatment
items.summary: Immuneel Therapeutics collaborates with Arogya Finance to unveil a zero-cost EMI scheme for advanced cancer treatments, providing support of up to Rs 25 lakh. This effort is a segment of Immuneel's Saksham Patient Access Program, letting eligible patients benefit from interest-free installments for as long as 12 months, thereby reducing financial pressures and facilitating prompt access to essential therapies for those in need.

items.title: LIC highlights stronger profitability, higher margins and digital push ahead of investor meetings
items.summary: India's Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) has reported robust financial performance, with net profit soaring to $6.07 billion and margins improving significantly to 21.2%. The state-owned giant continues its market dominance, holding a 56.7% share in new business premiums. A strategic shift towards higher-margin non-participating products is driving this growth, alongside a strong focus on digital transformation and expanding its vast distribution network.

items.title: India achieves major milestone: Helicopters can now land without radar as DGCA clears satellite-based navigation system
items.summary: India has achieved a significant aviation milestone with the approval of its first Private Point-in-Space (PinS) instrument approach procedure for helicopters at Undavalli Heliport. This satellite-based technology allows for safe and accurate landings, especially in challenging weather or remote locations. The innovation is set to boost helicopter operations across various sectors, enhancing safety and accessibility nationwide.

items.title: India aims to make it easier to short by nearly doubling stocks eligible for borrowing, sources say
items.summary: The three main criteria determining eligibility include liquidity, trading volume and the stock's ability to support exposure to derivatives trading.

items.title: Panattoni appoints Sharad Gohil as India CEO, MD
items.summary: Panattoni, a global industrial real estate leader, has appointed Sharad Gohil as CEO and MD for its India operations. With over 25 years of experience and a track record of significant transactions, Gohil will spearhead Panattoni's expansion in India's industrial, logistics, and data centre sectors.

items.title: Nomura names M&M, Hyundai, Ather Energy as top picks after June auto sales; says EV penetration at inflection point
items.summary: Nomura remains positive on India's automobile sector after strong June sales, saying electric vehicle adoption has reached an inflection point. The brokerage expects continued momentum driven by new launches and improving charging infrastructure, while naming Mahindra & Mahindra, Hyundai Motor India, Ather Energy and Sona BLW among its top picks.

items.title: CVC sells marina business for more than €1bn as yacht market booms
items.summary: French private equity firm InfraVia’s purchase of D-Marin is latest in a flurry of investments in sector

items.title: Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw flags off new train services in UP, UK and Maha; announces major rail push
items.summary: Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw launched new train services connecting Nanded to Mumbai and Tanakpur to Nanded, enhancing connectivity for the Sikh community and other regions. He also announced the extension of the Tanakpur-Pilibhit service to Shahjahanpur. The minister highlighted significant railway development over the past 12 years, including track expansion and electrification, with bullet train projects progressing rapidly. Emphasis remains on affordable travel for the common populace.

items.title: Sobha sets decent base for FY27 with record Q1 bookings
items.summary: Sobha's Q1FY27 bookings hit a record ₹3,656 crore, up over 70% sequentially and year-on-year, driven by strong response to its Hoskote luxury launch. The company is targeting 30% sales growth for the full year, even as rising supply and AI-linked job uncertainty pose watch-points ahead.

items.title: El Nino remains an important variable to watch; earnings recovery likely in H2FY27: Dharmesh Kakkad of ICICI Pru AMC
items.summary: Dharmesh Kakkad of ICICI Prudential AMC shares crucial insights on the impact of El Nino on India’s monsoon and discusses macroeconomic factors influencing market volatility.

items.title: FIIs bet on midcaps: 10 stocks that surged up to 70% in just 3 months
items.summary: Several midcap stocks delivered strong gains after FIIs increased their holdings in the March 2026 quarter. Relaxo Footwears, BHEL, Motilal Oswal Financial Services and Bank of Maharashtra were among the top performers, reflecting sustained foreign investor interest despite broader market volatility.

items.title: EasyJet reaches outline agreement on £5.5bn takeover by Castlelake
items.summary: UK airline’s board says it is minded to recommend proposal by US private credit group

items.title: NSE gears up to market $3-billion IPO next week
items.summary: NSE plans to hold investor meetings across the US as well as in London, Singapore, Hong Kong, the Middle East and India

items.title: 75% returns in YTD! Indiabulls share price hits a fresh 52-week high
items.summary: Indiabulls share price has delivered strong returns over a period time. The smallcap stock has gained 30% in one month and has delivered multibagger returns of 180% in three months. Indiabulls shares have jumped 104% in six months, while it has surged over 75% on a YTD basis.

items.title: Govt to launch cooperative life insurance company, expand Bharat Taxi to 500 cities: Amit Shah
items.summary: Union Minister Amit Shah announced a new cooperative life insurance company to bolster the sector's growth, marking the Ministry of Cooperation's 5th Foundation Day. He highlighted the ministry's role in revitalizing the cooperative movement. Initiatives like expanding 'Bharat Taxi' and establishing a Sahkari University were also discussed, aiming for a developed and self-reliant India by 2047.

items.title: Adani storms Hindalco-Vedanta duopoly with Rs 1.1 lakh crore gambit
items.summary: Gautam Adani's $11.5 billion aluminium joint venture with IHC marks his biggest bet yet on India's industrial future. Rather than challenging Hindalco and Vedanta directly, Adani is targeting soaring aluminium demand driven by manufacturing, renewable energy and infrastructure, aiming to build a fully integrated metals business and reshape India's aluminium industry.

items.title: Obsession, the steady rise of ‘horror-coated’ drama films and their quiet takeover of the mid-budget theatrical space
items.summary: Low-budget horror film 'Obsession' has shattered expectations, raking in over $400 million worldwide and becoming a top-grossing horror flick. This success highlights a growing trend: 'horror-coated' dramas that blend supernatural scares with compelling dramatic themes. These films are increasingly filling the mid-budget theatrical space, offering audiences a unique emotional buffer and proving the horror genre's versatility.

items.title: 10 Nifty stocks Motilal Oswal recommends buying ahead of Q1 results. What can shareholders expect?
items.summary: As the Q1FY27 earnings season begins with TCS's results on Thursday, Motilal Oswal expects Nifty earnings to grow 10%, the fastest pace in four quarters. The brokerage has identified 10 stocks, including Bharti Airtel, SBI, ICICI Bank, M&M and Titan, that it believes offer attractive upside ahead of the June quarter earnings, backed by strong fundamentals and growth prospects.

items.title: India's power demand likely to grow 6% annually as renewable capacity additions stay strong
items.summary: India's power demand is expected to grow at around 6 per cent annually over the next four to five years, supported by continued renewable energy capacity additions of 45-50 GW every year, according to a Centrum Institutional Research Q1FY27 power sector results preview report.

items.title: Why did market rise today? Sensex gains 520 points, Nifty closes above 24,400. 6 key factors
items.summary: Indian stock markets surged for a fourth straight day, driven by renewed monsoon hopes following heavy rainfall and a return of foreign institutional investors as net buyers. Key indices Sensex and Nifty saw significant gains, boosting market capitalization. Positive sentiment was further fueled by easing geopolitical tensions and falling oil prices, with heavyweight stocks leading the advance. Investors now await Q1 earnings reports for further market direction.

items.title: Global Market: ECB says Target 2 payment system back to normal after brief processing disruption
items.summary: The European Central Bank said its Target 2 (T2) payment system resumed normal operations after a brief technical disruption delayed payment processing. The incident temporarily affected the settlement window for euro and Danish krone transactions before the ECB confirmed the issue had been resolved.

items.title: Knack Packaging IPO allotment date today. GMP, steps to check share allotment status online on BSE, NSE and MUFG Intime
items.summary: Knack Packaging IPO allotment date is likely today, 6 July 2026. Investors can check Knack Packaging IPO allotment status online through the websites of BSE and NSE, along with the official portal of the IPO registrar. MUFG Intime India is the Knack Packaging IPO registrar.

items.title: Global Market: European shares steady as investors track deal activity; easyJet jumps on takeover proposal
items.summary: European shares held steady as dealmaking took center stage, with a potential takeover of easyJet by Castlelake boosting travel stocks. Declining oil prices also supported sentiment, though geopolitical tensions remain a concern. Ferrari's new manual gearbox sports car launch pleased enthusiasts, while Thales' drone acquisition plans were noted. Investors are cautiously weighing corporate news against ongoing global uncertainties.

items.title: Jefferies' Christopher Wood warns Microsoft, Meta, and Alphabet AI spending may backfire
items.summary: Jefferies strategist Chris Wood has warned that Wall Street's hyperscalers risk massive capital destruction from excessive AI spending, arguing markets may soon push back against rising debt-funded investments. He also flagged geopolitical risks and drew attention to growing concerns that AI valuations could become detached from economic fundamentals.

items.title: Kusumgar IPO GMP signals 41% listing pop: 10 key things to know from the RHP ahead of offer launch on Wednesday, 8 July
items.summary: As Kusumgar prepares for its IPO launch on July 8, the grey market hints at a promising 41% premium. With insights from its Red Herring Prospectus, discover vital information about the company’s operations and financials.

items.title: Stocks to Watch Today: Supriya Lifescience, Rubicon Research, Fortis Healthcare, Fermenta Biotech
items.summary: Customs Department has initiated probe against its general manager, sales and marketing officer Sreekant Sreedharan of Supriya Life

items.title: 15 stocks to Watch July 6: CG Power , NTPC Green, Oswal Pumps, Jyoti, NBCC, Prestige Estates, Stanley Lifestyle
items.summary: Focus will also be won GK Energy, Swelect Energy, CFF Fluid, Advent Hotels, Krystal Integrated, Enviro Infra Engineers, Sayaji Hotels, Ameenji Rubbe

items.title: AU Small Finance Bank's growth engine is intact, but questions remain
items.summary: Whether the momentum in the lender's mainstay vehicle financing has sustained in Q1 amid rising crude oil and commodity costs and a fading GST 2.0 push has to be seen.

items.title: NSE IPO: Bourse plans to launch $3 billion issue in September; marketing likely to begin next week: Report
items.summary: NSE IPO: The National Stock Exchange will begin formal marketing for its IPO next week, aiming for a September listing. Targeting $3 billion, it has appointed 20 banks for the sale. The IPO structure is an offer-for-sale benefiting existing shareholders, differing from other major listings.

items.title: South Korean prosecutors indict four major refiners over allegations of oil price collusion
items.summary: South Korean prosecutors on Monday indicted four major domestic oil refiners for violating fair trade laws.

items.title: Bitcoin approaches $63,000 after $224 million ETF inflows snap six-day outflow streak
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $63,000 after nearly $224 million in ETF inflows ended a six-day outflow streak, supporting a mild recovery in crypto markets. Ethereum and major altcoins also gained, while analysts said sentiment is improving, but caution remains ahead of upcoming macroeconomic events, including the FOMC minutes.

items.title: HDFC Bank vs Yes Bank vs Kotak Bank vs IndusInd Bank - Which banking stock to buy after Q1 business update?
items.summary: Shares of HDFC Bank and Axis Bank gained as much as 2.5% on Monday, while Kotak Mahindra Bank declined up to 3.5%.

items.title: El Nino threatens to fuel coal power surge in India, study says
items.summary: A potential super El Niño could put more pressure on India's power grid than any other country's and slow its transition away from coal, according to a new report by the Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA).

items.title: FPI Trends: Financials, construction lead inflows in second half of June; Metals, power see highest outflows
items.summary: FPI Trends: The financial services sector emerged as the biggest beneficiary of FPI buying in the second half of June, attracting net inflows of ₹14,634 crore, reversing net outflows of ₹11,263 crore recorded during the first half of the month.

items.title: Suzuki Motor arm Next Bharat Ventures' ₹2,000 crore second impact fund to back rural entrepreneurs
items.summary: Next Bharat Venture’s second fund will focus on agriculture, financial inclusion, healthcare, and AI for social good among others.

items.title: Sensex touches 78,370, Nifty tops 24,450 as bank stocks, lower crude lift markets
items.summary: Most sectoral indices remained in positive territory, led by the realty, oil & gas and auto indices, which rose more than 1% each; The Nifty Media index declined more than 1%

items.title: Ashok Leyland partners with Rosmerta Recycling to boost commercial vehicle scrapping
items.summary: Ashok Leyland has joined forces with Rosmerta Recycling to streamline the scrapping of old commercial vehicles across India. This partnership will offer customers a hassle-free process, including deregistration support, at authorized facilities. The initiative aligns with government programs to modernize fleets, reduce pollution, and encourage the adoption of newer, eco-friendly vehicles, providing customers with benefits like discounts and tax waivers.

items.title: Aastha Spintex share price hits 5% upper circuit after weak debut. Buy, sell or hold?
items.summary: Aastha Spintex shares surged 5% on July 6 after a weak debut at ₹130, below the IPO price of ₹136. The IPO was oversubscribed 4.64 times, aiming to raise ₹170 crore, with plans to use funds for acquiring Falcon Yarns and working capital.

items.title: Why Iran may find it difficult to clear its oil inventories even after sanctions relief
items.summary: Iran may face challenges in clearing oil inventories even after restrictions have been lifted, amid oil supplies elsewhere while China becomes less enthusiastic

items.title: VB-G RAM G: A potential rural income hike comes with a burden
items.summary: India's new rural employment scheme, VB-G RAM G, promises more work and higher wages, aiming to boost rural incomes. However, a significant shift in funding responsibility to states raises concerns. Many states could face a four to five-fold increase in their financial contribution, potentially forcing difficult budgetary trade-offs and impacting the scheme's overall effectiveness in reviving the rural economy.

items.title: What Sky buying ITV could mean for your favourite shows
items.summary: Sky is set to buy ITV's TV and streaming channels for up to £1.6bn, the companies announced on Monday.

items.title: Your mobile bills may go up by another 12-15% soon
items.summary: Get ready for another telecom tariff hike, with prices expected to rise by 12-15% in the next three to four months. This comes as the market consolidates, favoring established players. Expect a slight increase in average revenue per user this quarter, driven by upgrades to 4G and 5G. Meanwhile, Reliance Jio and Bharti Airtel are set to gain more subscribers, while Vodafone Idea continues its 5G expansion.

items.title: Jewellery stock Senco Gold jumps over 6% after Q1FY27 business update
items.summary: Senco Gold share price rose over 6% on July 6 after reporting strong Q1 FY27 results, driven by festive demand and retail expansion. The company opened eight new showrooms, achieving 60% year-on-year revenue growth despite challenges from higher gold prices and customs duties.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Trump sees red, prediction markets score and NATO's defensive
items.summary: The World Cup takes an extraordinary turn after President Trump's reported red card intervention.

items.title: Adon Agro Commodities share price hits upper circuit after listing at a 12% premium; details here
items.summary: Adon Agro Commodities shares are listed at ₹78.25 on the BSE SME, up almost 12% over the issue price of ₹70. The SME stock extended gains and hit its upper price band of ₹82.16, up more than 17% from the issue price.

items.title: True North cashes out $2 billion for investors as PE firms step up exits
items.summary: This comes on the back of exits for True North from portfolio firms like FedFina, Niva Bupa, Biocon Biologics, Infinity Fincorp Solutions, Zydus Wellness and Home First Finance.

items.title: Shakti Pumps India share price jumps 5% after receipt of order worth over ₹350 crore
items.summary: Shakti Pumps India share price opened at ₹607.95 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹574.10 on Friday. The stock touched an intraday high of ₹621.90 on 6 July.

items.title: Epigamia to expand production capacity as value-added dairy market heats up
items.summary: Epigamia is expanding manufacturing capacity as its new CEO and COO settle in following 50% growth over two years, with Greek yogurt still generating more than half of company's revenue.

items.summary: JP Morgan sees gold averaging $4,300/oz in Q3 2026

items.title: Laser Power & Infra sets IPO price band at ₹203-214/share; issue opens July 9
items.summary: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of equity shares worth ₹542 crore and an Offer for Sale (OFS) of shares aggregating ₹200 crore by promoters

items.title: Meta’s woes deepen in India as child abuse ads on Instagram draw government ire
items.summary: U.S. social media giant Meta is facing an onslaught of regulatory scrutiny in India, which has the largest user base for Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp.

items.title: Small-cap stock under ₹50 jumps after this AI update
items.summary: Small-cap stock under ₹50: Blue Cloud Softech Solutions share price rallied 20% in one month and has gained 13% in three months. The smallcap stock has risen just 3% in six months, while it has declined 37% in one year.

items.title: Why has Nifty 50 delivered zero returns over the last 2 years? It's not just AI and geopolitics. Explained
items.summary: The Indian stock market has underperformed most global markets over the past three years. Nifty 50 has delivered zero returns over the last two years.

items.title: How the Iran crisis undermines the Bank’s QT programme
items.summary: Unlike the previous crises, this time the Bank carried on selling. It is time for a rethink.

items.title: 'NATO 3.0': Defense spending pledges face the Trump test
items.summary: Leaders are expected to focus on whether Europe can turn higher defense spending into military power as Washington pushes allies to shoulder more of the burden.

items.title: Ebola outbreak containment hampered by USAID closure, experts say
items.summary: Foreign aid cuts have "demonstrably worsened" the Ebola crisis, a virologist told CNBC.

items.title: HarperCollins acquires rights to Rajiv Bakshi's debut book on executive success
items.summary: HarperCollins India has secured the global rights to Rajiv Bakshi's first literary work, which serves as a practical roadmap for achieving success in the modern corporate landscape. Leveraging his vast experience in leadership, Bakshi's book addresses essential decisions and habits for both burgeoning and seasoned professionals. The ultimate goal is to arm readers with the assurance needed to fulfill their professional ambitions.

items.title: Risk-on sentiment to keep Indian market on consolidation
items.summary: Ponmudi R, CEO - Enrich Money, said Indian equities are expected to open on a steady note, supported by positive global cues, subdued crude oil prices and improving risk sentiment

items.title: Glenmark launches generic Olanzapine injection in US
items.summary: Glenmark Pharmaceuticals has launched a generic version of Olanzapine injection in the United States, offering a treatment for schizophrenia and bipolar disorder. This move by its US arm, Glenmark Pharmaceuticals Inc., introduces a bioequivalent alternative to Zyprexa intramuscular injection. The company aims to expand its injectable offerings and bolster its presence in the institutional market, targeting a segment with significant annual sales.

items.title: Rupee falls 10 paise to 95.28 against US dollar in early trade
items.summary: Forex traders say RBI may use fresh foreign inflows to rebuild its reserve position rather than allowing the rupee to strengthen too much

items.title: Standard Chartered, BlackRock launch Asia Pacific-focused multi-asset fund
items.summary: The fund will ​be made available to accredited and professional ⁠investors across the bank's priority, priority private and ‌private banking segments

items.title: Aastha Spintex share price makes weak debut, lists at 4% discount to the IPO price
items.summary: Aastha Spintex share price listed at 4.41% discount to the IPO price of ₹136 per share. The stock opened at ₹130 per share on both NSE and BSE.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty open higher as HDFC Bank, Axis Bank rally; lower crude aids sentiment
items.summary: Among the Nifty 50 stocks, Bharat Electronics, HDFC Bank, Axis Bank and Hindalco were the top gainers; Kotak Mahindra Bank, Power Grid, TCS and Bajaj Finserv were among the major laggards

items.title: HAL, BEL to Zen Technologies: Defence stocks rally on ₹52,000 crore procurement boost; check key beneficiaries
items.summary: Zen Technologies emerged as the top gainer on the Nifty India Defence index, surging over 4%, followed by Paras Defence and Space Technologies, Bharat Dynamics, Bharat Electronics (BEL), Data Patterns (India), and Solar Industries.

items.title: Healthcare stock Unihealth Hospitals hits 5% upper circuit; here's why
items.summary: Unihealth Hospitals shares hit the 5% upper circuit after announcing a 49.81% stake acquisition in Uganda's Victoria Hospital, boosting its ownership to 99.81%. This strategic move supports its international expansion goals and ensures future economic benefits directly benefit its shareholders.

items.title: One tycoon makes millions from 'dark' journeys on a dangerous oil route while others feared Hormuz
items.summary: Amidst the Persian Gulf war, the UAE secretly navigated its oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz using 'dark' ship transits. Korean shipping tycoon Ga-Hyun Chung's Sinokor Group played a pivotal role, leasing a significant portion of vessels for these risky "shuttle runs." This covert operation allowed the UAE to maintain pre-war export levels, capitalizing on surging oil prices and creating immense profits for Sinokor and its partners.

items.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Dharmesh Shah of ICICI Sec suggests buying Shriram Finance, Piramal Pharma shares on 6 July
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices, Sensex and Nifty 50, opened higher on July 6, driven by gains in private banking stocks following strong quarterly updates from HDFC Bank and Axis Bank. Market breadth was positive with 14 of the 16 major indices in the green.

items.title: Bond traders watch for treasury auctions, June Fed minutes
items.summary: The sale of benchmark 10-year notes comes Wednesday, followed by 30-year bonds a day later as part of the Treasury’s $119 billion week of auctions. It kicks off Tuesday with 3-year notes

items.title: Why OpenAI and Anthropic may struggle to float

items.title: HDFC Bank share price rises over 3.5% on strong Q1 business update. Do you own?
items.summary: HDFC Bank shares gained 3.6% to ₹823, backed by solid growth in loans and deposits.

items.title: Gold, silver trade volatile amid elevated dollar, easing US Fed rate hike bets; what should investors do?
items.summary: Gold and silver prices witnessed some volatility in early trade on the MCX on Monday, 6 July, torn between easing concerns over US Fed rate hikes after softer-than-expected jobs data, and elevated dollar.

items.title: Rupee opens 2 paisa lower at 95.23 against US dollar
items.summary: The Indian rupee opened at 95.23 against the US dollar, pressured by strong dollar demand despite falling crude oil prices. Recent declines in the rupee and continued corporate dollar purchases suggest ongoing challenges, even with RBI interventions to stabilize the currency.

items.title: Laser Power & Infra IPO: Price band set at ₹203-214 per share; check key dates, issue details
items.summary: The Laser Power & Infra IPO will open for subscription on 9 July and close on 13 July, with a price band of ₹203 to ₹214. The company seeks to raise ₹742 crore, primarily for debt repayment and corporate purposes.

items.title: Nvidia's next-gen AI rack system delayed to 2028 on manufacturing snags, SemiAnalysis says
items.summary: The reported delay adds to concerns that Nvidia's breakneck annual release cadence is colliding with manufacturing limits.

items.title: Trump’s war means higher global interest rates for years to come
items.summary: Global interest rates are set to remain higher for longer, with Bloomberg Economics forecasting borrowing costs elevated by up to half a percentage point through 2028. This shift, influenced by lingering inflation risks from the Iran conflict's energy shock and the AI race, means consumers and businesses face more expensive loans. Central banks, wary of past inflation surges, are maintaining hawkish stances, impacting economies worldwide.

items.title: High jewellery boosts desirability to outshine luxury sector
items.summary: Asset-rich buyers, soaring gold prices and big launches have raised perceptions of the category’s investment potential

items.title: Backlash after China bubble tea firm ordered to pay Louis Vuitton $1.5m
items.summary: A court in China ruled that Molly Tea had infringed on the luxury brand's four-petal flower design.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Red cards, record bets and market's risk-on mood
items.summary: Drama is not only on the pitch — it is showing up across prediction markets, oil and Wall Street's risk appetite.

items.title: Lenders temper Vi business plan, seek group's guarantor for massive loan
items.summary: Lenders are prevailing upon the company to scale back its financial projections, while also demanding a corporate guarantee from another Aditya Birla Group firm, according to two people aware of the development.

items.title: Three things you can do to stop EU border checks at the airport costing you
items.summary: Queues are expected at airports this summer owing to EU's new digital border control system.

items.title: UK regulator warns of ‘arms race’ to keep up with AI use in financial services

items.title: India and Russia set $50 billion investment target by 2030
items.summary: India and Russia are targeting $50 billion in mutual investments by 2030, focusing on joint ventures in advanced manufacturing, critical minerals, and green technology. A recent meeting in Moscow saw both nations agreeing to encourage private sector exploration and collaboration. India highlighted its cost-effective skilled workforce and investment-friendly reforms, while the "Making in Russia for India" model was also promoted to boost bilateral industrial ties.

items.title: Concerns rise over low procurement for onion buffer stock
items.summary: Government efforts to build a buffer stock of onions are lagging significantly, with less than 5% of the target procured despite multiple price hikes. This slow pace raises concerns about market stability and potential hoarding, especially with the upcoming festive season and weather uncertainties impacting the next crop. Farmers are reportedly selling at higher market rates, bypassing government procurement.

items.title: Fall in global crude prices brightens fiscal outlook, El Nino risks linger
items.summary: Easing West Asian tensions have significantly improved India's fiscal outlook, with economists now predicting a smaller deficit than previously feared. Lower global crude oil prices are reducing the government's subsidy burden and boosting revenue prospects. While subsidy pressures and potential agricultural impacts remain concerns, the improved energy price scenario offers a more favorable fiscal path for the nation.

items.title: 'America's still at the top': Why Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas says US is best place to build a startup
items.summary: Perplexity CEO and co-founder Aravind Srinivas says he always thought America's the only country where a person can have an idea, and people will listen to it and encourage to pursue it

items.title: Europe’s richest man Bernard Arnault told to pay nearly ₹245 crore in taxes – How much is net worth of LVMH founder?

items.title: Nykaa expects Q1 revenue growth near 30% on fashion rebound

items.title: Tesla puts the brakes on AI costs, caps staff spending at ₹19,000 weekly, starting July 6
items.summary: Tesla is limiting staff spending on AI tools to ₹19,000 per week starting July 6, requiring manager approval for more, after some engineers reportedly spent thousands on AI tokens each week.

items.title: Govt extends deadline for WhatsApp's response over username issue to July 9: Report
items.summary: The mater pertains to security concerns over Meta-owned platform's recently announced usernames feature that will allow users to communicate without sharing their phone numbers.

items.title: Falling oil prices give RBI more room to focus on growth, but relief uneven across Asia: Standard Chartered
items.summary: Falling crude oil prices are providing a significant boost to India and other Asian nations, allowing central banks to prioritize economic growth. This "disinflationary relief" eases pressure on external accounts, especially for heavy energy importers like India, Thailand, and the Philippines. While some economies like South Korea may still face inflation due to AI booms, the overall trend favors easing.

items.title: Mint Explainer: How do sovereign guarantees work and why does India use them?
items.summary: Disclosed annually in the Union budget, these multi-crore commitments act as a crucial safety net for statutory corporations, though they carry potential default risks for the national exchequer.

items.title: ICICI Bank, Prudential sign undertaking to defuse conflict-of-interest concerns ahead of Bharti Life deal
items.summary: The undertaking, signed between the two promoters of ICICI Prudential Life on Saturday, seeks to address potential conflicts of interest arising from Prudential's proposed acquisition of Bharti Life.

items.title: Purabi Dairy, NDDB and Suzuki R&D India sign MoU to establish compressed biogas plant in Assam
items.summary: Assam is set to boost sustainable development with a new compressed biogas plant. The National Dairy Development Board, Suzuki R&D India, and North East Dairy and Foods Ltd. have partnered to convert agricultural and dairy waste into clean biogas and organic fertiliser. This initiative promises to enhance rural livelihoods, promote scientific waste management, and contribute to cleaner mobility, aligning with the state's green goals and fostering a circular economy.

items.title: India's outbound travel defies West Asia turmoil as Thailand, Australia, Singapore and US woo tourists
items.summary: Indian outbound tourism is showing no signs of slowing. Thailand has recorded 1.19 million Indian arrivals in 2026, while Singapore has received nearly half a million visitors.

items.title: Aluminium industry divided over removing custom duties for scraps and finished products
items.summary: India's aluminium industry is split over import duties. Primary producers, represented by the Aluminium Association of India (AAI), want stricter controls on aluminium scrap imports and quality regulations, arguing that rising imports of allegedly substandard scrap could threaten more than ₹3 lakh crore of planned investments.

items.title: Community apps find new ways to cash in on gated communities
items.summary: Firms like MyGate, Nobrokerhood and Park+ are using their access to affluent gated communities using divergent strategies to build sustainable businesses beyond subscription models.

items.title: Clay, kilns and the cost of survival for tile makers
items.summary: While some firms lean into their heritage, others are modernising in the face of economic pressures.

items.title: Suvendu Adhikari announces Rs 313 crore Pradhan Mantri Cha Shramik Protsahan Yojana for West Bengal tea workers
items.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced the Rs 313.30 crore Pradhan Mantri Cha Shramik Protsahan Yojana (PMCSPY) to boost education, healthcare, and welfare for North Bengal's tea garden workers. The scheme allocates Rs 177 crore for education, Rs 72 crore for healthcare, and Rs 63 crore for 321 resting sheds. This initiative aims for comprehensive development and well-being of these workers and their families.

items.title: How Bending Spoons built a $23bn tech empire from struggling brands
items.summary: Nasdaq listing caps more than a decade of deals that transformed an Italian start-up into a global internet company

items.title: Oil market opens up as retail traders pour in
items.summary: CME Group to launch 10-barrel contract after Iran war triggers rush of bets on crude prices

items.title: High share prices are becoming a status symbol
items.summary: Decline in stock splits suggests dividing share count no longer rules corporate orthodoxy

items.title: Is Greaves Cotton's diversification strategy starting to pay off?
items.summary: The engineering company says businesses spanning energy, industrial solutions and exports are reducing its reliance on diesel three-wheeler engines, while acquisitions remain on the agenda.

items.title: India tightens scrutiny of Chinese herbicide imports
items.summary: India is intensifying its watch on glufosinate imports from China, a crucial herbicide. Authorities are investigating if Chinese exporters are trying to bypass anti-dumping duties by adjusting prices. This move aims to protect domestic manufacturers. Meanwhile, anti-dumping duties on butyl alcohol from the US, Malaysia and South Africa have been extended.

items.title: India-Israel investment pact comes into effect
items.summary: India and Israel's new investment pact is now active, aiming to boost trade beyond $1 billion by offering quicker dispute resolution and enhanced investor protection. This agreement, signed in September 2025, allows a three-year window for domestic legal remedies before international arbitration, while safeguarding India's policy-making autonomy. It covers a broad range of investments and replaces a previous accord.

items.title: Akasa Air expands fleet to 40 aircraft with delivery of ninth Boeing 737 MAX in 2026
items.summary: The aircraft was ferried to India via a multi-stop route that began in Seattle, United States, continued through Reykjavik, Iceland, and Cairo, Egypt, before completing its final journey to Bengaluru

items.title: I’ve worked with over 5,000 kids: I swear by this No. 1 parenting rule—it’s ‘surprisingly simple’
items.summary: Child development expert Siggie Cohen, who has worked with over 5,000 families, shares one communication mistake she sees parents make every day. Here's why she says it can backfire, when to set clear boundaries and how to use questions more effectively.

items.title: Finance Ministry notifies rules for determination of origin of goods under India-UK trade pact
items.summary: India's Finance Ministry has notified rules for determining the origin of goods under the India-UK CETA, effective July 15, 2026. This agreement grants duty-free access for 99% of Indian exports to the UK, boosting sectors like textiles and engineering. The rules ensure only genuine goods qualify for preferential tariffs, preventing misuse and strengthening the trade pact's integrity. Businesses must now align supply chains with these origin criteria for full benefit.

items.title: OpenAI mulls 5% US government stake to share AI wealth with Americans: Report
items.summary: Such a move, if eventually accepted by the Trump administration, could create an unusual relationship between the government and one of the world's leading AI companies
